baseline.commit_subject: Add Mira Core Phase 23 milestone readiness validation
baseline.baseline_boundary: Phase 23 is a current read-only rollup over Phases 1-22. It does not create a real runtime, server, listener, network path, database/store/file write, migration, queue, lease, auth/session/key/secret operation, local execution, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, export, delete, restore, raw-content storage, state persistence, audit-log write, or output-file behavior.

purpose: Define the exact operator-visible gates that must be true before any future real Mira Core runtime/server/listener/network/store/queue/lease/execution/output-file behavior can be implemented. Phase 24 is a contract and validation surface only; it must not promote runtime behavior by itself.
nonGoals: No runtime implementation in this Oracle fixture.
No real server, daemon, listener, route, HTTP, WebSocket, or network behavior.
No database, store, file, migration, queue, lease, or output-file writes.
No auth, token, session, signing, key, encryption, KMS, secret, or env-secret operation.
No local execution, shell, PTY, browser, screenshot, OCR, or window access.
No customer sends, external sends, deploys, trades, financial actions, export, delete, restore, capture, raw-content storage, source-store writes, memory/profile commits, or durable state changes.
No claim that socket connection, delivery acceptance, handler response, harness validation, local acceptance, or this promotion gate proves bridge green or model processing.
No eligibility decision may be treated as authorization to implement a runtime slice without a later explicit Builder task and review.

scope: Build a local validation-only runtime_promotion_gate_report and validation_report against this Oracle fixture.
Pin baseline commit 3ce041c and depend on Phase 23 milestone readiness being current through Phase 22.
Expose an operator-visible promotion decision with exactly one of remain_validation_only, blocked, or eligible_for_future_runtime_slice.
Default safe output should remain_validation_only unless all future-runtime prerequisites are explicitly represented as satisfied by references.
Treat eligible_for_future_runtime_slice as future planning only; it must not start, bind, route, store, queue, lease, execute, send, deploy, trade, or write an output file.

Require explicit runtime mode flag, local-only development binding, kill switch, dry-run fallback, auth/signing/key refs from Phases 15-19, API/handler/harness refs from Phases 20-22, and storage/audit boundaries from Phases 17-18 before future eligibility.
Preserve proof boundaries: socket is not bridge green, delivery acceptance is not model-processing proof, server cannot execute local arms, and direct server targeting of Builder/Oracle is blocked.
Preserve replay/idempotency/tombstone/watermark/expiry gates and require Phase 22 ORACLE #78/#79/#80 closure.
Represent future environment/config gates by reference only: port binding, auth secret presence, storage path, migration plan, telemetry/audit plan, rollback plan, and disable plan.
Reject overclaiming eligibility, hidden network/listener/store behavior, unsafe Tier2/Tier3/Tier4 promotion, raw/private/secret leakage, fake proof, and missing kill switch/rollback/audit gates.
Emit side_effect_result showing no runtime/server/listener/routes/network/db/store/file/migration/queue/lease/auth/key/secret/local execution/send/deploy/trade/output-file behavior now.
hardRejects: Any real runtime, server, daemon, listener, route, HTTP/WebSocket, network, database/store/file write, migration, queue, lease, auth/session/key/secret operation, local execution, shell/PTY, browser/window access, send, deploy, trade, export, delete, restore, raw-content storage, source-store write, memory/profile commit, or output-file behavior occurs.
Promotion decision is eligible_for_future_runtime_slice while any required prerequisite gate is missing, blocked, unknown, stale, or represented by raw secret content instead of a reference.
Promotion decision is eligible_for_future_runtime_slice for Tier2 repo mutation, Tier3 external side effect, Tier4 financial/irreversible action, deploy, customer-send, trade, or local execution.
Manifest claims socket connection is bridge green, delivery acceptance is model-processing proof, server can execute local arms, or Builder/Oracle are valid direct server targets.
Phase 23 milestone readiness is absent, stale, not baseline 3ce041c, or does not cover Phases 1-22.
Phase 22 ORACLE #78/#79/#80 closure is missing or replay/idempotency/tombstone/watermark/expiry gates are omitted.
Kill switch, dry-run fallback, rollback/disable plan, telemetry/audit plan, local-only binding, or explicit runtime mode flag is missing.

Raw private content, raw comms, terminal scrollback, screenshot/OCR/browser state, customer private content, side-profile content, source-store payloads, tokens, cookies, keys, private keys, data keys, secrets, plaintext, ciphertext, or decrypted payloads appear in output.
Validation report fakes test proof, runtime proof, bridge proof, or model-processing proof.

staticValidationRules.rule: Runtime promotion gate report and validation report must pin baseline_commit to 3ce041c.
Phase 23 milestone readiness dependency must be present, baseline 3ce041c, current through Phase 22, phase inventory count 22, and Phase 13 stale/superseded.

Promotion decision must be one of remain_validation_only, blocked, or eligible_for_future_runtime_slice; eligible may appear only with every prerequisite gate satisfied and still performs no side effects.
Explicit runtime mode flag, local-only binding, kill switch, dry-run fallback, Phase 15-22 refs, and Phase 23 dependency must be represented before future eligibility.
All real runtime/server/listener/route/network/db/store/file/migration/queue/lease/auth/key/secret/local-execution/send/deploy/trade/output-file behavior is blocked now.
Socket is not bridge green, delivery acceptance is not model-processing proof, server cannot execute local arms, and Builder/Oracle direct server targets remain blocked.
Replay, idempotency, tombstone, watermark, expiry, same-sequence binding, recomputed-idempotency regressions, and Phase 22 ORACLE #78/#79/#80 closure are required.
Future port binding, auth secret presence, storage path, migration, telemetry/audit, rollback, and disable plans are represented by references only with no raw secret or raw path content.
Tier2 repo mutation, Tier3 external side effect, Tier4 financial/irreversible, customer-send, deploy, trade, and local execution cannot be promoted.

Output must not contain raw private content, raw comms, terminal scrollback, screenshot/OCR/browser state, customer private content, side-profile content, tokens, cookies, keys, secrets, plaintext, ciphertext, or decrypted payloads.
Output must not claim bridge green, model-processing proof, runtime proof, command proof, or eligibility proof without explicit evidence refs and safe boundary flags.
Missing kill switch, dry-run fallback, rollback/disable plan, or telemetry/audit plan blocks future runtime eligibility.
Equivalent inputs should produce stable idempotency keys; dependency, decision, prerequisite, proof, replay, environment, or side-effect gate changes must reject or change idempotency.

acceptanceChecks.focus: Phase 24 depends on Phase 23 milestone readiness current through Phase 22.
Promotion decision is operator-visible and restricted to the allowed decision enum.
Future runtime eligibility requires explicit mode, local-only bind, kill switch, dry-run fallback, auth/signing/key refs, server API/handler/harness refs, and storage/audit refs.
Socket/delivery/runtime gate proof boundaries and target limits stay false.
Replay/idempotency/tombstone/watermark/expiry gates and ORACLE #78/#79/#80 closure remain required.
Reject eligible_for_future_runtime_slice when prerequisites, kill switch, rollback, audit, proof, or side-effect gates are incomplete.
Reject Tier2/Tier3/Tier4 promotion, customer-send, deploy, trade, or local execution.
Reject socket, delivery, handler, harness, or promotion gate proof overclaims.
Missing kill switch, dry-run fallback, rollback/disable plan, or telemetry/audit plan blocks future eligibility.
Idempotency is stable for equivalent inputs and sensitive to dependency, decision, prerequisite, proof, replay, environment, and side-effect gate changes.

tamperCases.mutation: Set operator_promotion_decision.decision=eligible_for_future_runtime_slice while operator-kill-switch is missing or blocked.
Set listener/network/store side-effect counters above zero or side-effect truth false.
Set promotion risk tier to tier3_external_side_effect or add deploy/customer-send/trade action class.
Insert raw auth secret, bearer token, private key, plaintext payload, ciphertext payload, or decrypted payload into an output string value.
Use Phase 13 readiness or a Phase 23 dependency not current through Phase 22.

summaryAcceptanceCriteria: Fixture is validation/spec-only and pins baseline 3ce041c.
Phase 23 milestone readiness dependency is current through Phase 22 and baseline 3ce041c.
Operator-visible decision shape allows remain_validation_only, blocked, or eligible_for_future_runtime_slice; default remains validation-only.

Future eligibility requires explicit runtime mode flag, local-only dev binding, kill switch, dry-run fallback, Phase 15-22 refs, Phase 23 current readiness, storage/audit boundaries, and reference-only environment/config gates.
No real runtime, server, listener, route, network, database, store, file, migration, queue, lease, auth, key, secret, local execution, send, deploy, trade, or output-file behavior occurs.
Proof boundaries preserve socket-not-bridge-green, delivery-not-model-processing-proof, server-cannot-execute-local-arms, and Builder/Oracle direct server targets blocked.
Replay/idempotency/tombstone/watermark/expiry gates and Phase 22 ORACLE #78/#79/#80 closure remain required.
Future environment/config requirements are reference-only and never expose raw secrets or raw local paths.

Tamper coverage rejects eligibility overclaim, hidden listener/network/store behavior, unsafe Tier2/Tier3/Tier4 promotion, raw/private/secret leakage, fake proof, and missing kill switch/rollback/audit gates.
Next work remains a validator-only implementation or future spec-only slice until Architect explicitly delegates runtime work after all gates are proven.
